description: Negative-consent model. Each session the AI plans the next 12 hours, emails a reviewable plan, and executes all safe, semi-safe, and reversible high-risk work automatically unless the owner stops or modifies a specific item via Telegram. Only irreversible/destructive task types in 'require_explicit_approval' need a positive go-ahead.

review_model.auto_execute: Everything listed in the emailed plan runs automatically during the session window unless the owner intervenes via Telegram.
owner_controls: stop <TASK_ID> â€” cancel a planned or running item
change <TASK_ID> to <instruction> â€” modify an item before it runs
approve <TASK_ID> â€” green-light an item in require_explicit_approval
pause â€” halt all autonomous execution until resumed
review_model.plan_email: Sent at the start of each session (8:00 AM and 8:00 PM {{TIMEZONE_ABBR}}), listing everything that will run in the next 12 hours.

require_explicit_approval_note: Irreversible or destructive. These NEVER auto-run under the opt-out model â€” they are listed under 'needs_explicit_go' in the plan email and require an explicit 'approve <TASK_ID>' via Telegram (high-risk pipeline Phase 1). Reversible high-impact changes (homepage_edit, navigation_change, redirect_setup, new_service_page, schema_removal) ARE auto-approved: the pipelines publish them straight to production (main), the owner reviews the live site daily before search engines index, and deploy-rollback reverts anything unwanted. There is NO preview/human-merge gate â€” do not plan work that depends on one.

execution_routing.note: Two consumer lanes execute approved work; the planner assigns risk_level/task_type so each task lands in a lane that can actually finish it. NEVER create work that has no finishing executor (that is what caused empty preview branches + zombie 'preview_validated' tasks).

deterministic_ops.deploys: straight to production via safe-fix (safe and semi_safe both â†’ safe-fix --production)
deterministic_ops.cwv_note: cwv_fix/page_speed_fix are deterministic page-speed edits the safe executor applies itself: lazy-load below-the-fold images (everything after the first H2; LCP candidates above it are never touched) and defer render-blocking external <head> scripts. Use when tech-health/speed-audit reports CWV regressions on a page. Anything deeper (image re-encoding, critical CSS, third-party scripts) is NOT deterministic — plan it as a content/Hermes task instead. These open a 'performance'-lever measurement window like any other ranking-affecting deploy, which prevents a speed problem from being misattributed to a content change.
deterministic_ops.handles_note: ranking_followup is self-scheduled by the executor after a ranking-affecting deploy (deferred ~14d via scheduled_for). It judges outcome on GSC clicks (primary; see outcome_loop), and on a CONFIRMED regression (debounced — 2 consecutive degraded checks) auto-remediates: rollback for metadata/link levers, content_refresh enqueue for content levers, with a ranking_recovery fallback for a human. The planner does not need to create these.
deterministic_ops.risk_guidance: Assign these risk_level=safe (or semi_safe). Both auto-deploy to production with no gate.

hermes_content.deploys: straight to production (main) via a fresh Hermes authoring/refresh session
handles: service_page_gap + money_page_refresh (edit_refresh_needed â€” rewrites/fixes an EXISTING page in place)
hermes_content.risk_guidance: Any task that REWRITES or AUTHORS page copy is a content task â€” route it by task_type to this lane. Do NOT mark page-rewrite work as a generic high_risk ops task; the ops safe executor has no content handler and the high-risk pipeline will mark it 'blocked'.

explicit_approval.flow: high-risk Phase 1 â†’ waiting_for_approval â†’ owner 'approve <id>' via Telegram

research_window.note: A ranking-affecting deploy opens a measurement window (experiments row) on its URL+lever and schedules a ~14d ranking_followup that closes it with a clicks-primary outcome (see outcome_loop). While open, routeTask parks a SAME-lever change to that URL in the 'research_hold' bucket (flag research_hold_same_lever) so it never confounds attribution; task-next/task-audit treat research_hold as non-executable. The hold auto-lifts at the experiment's ended_at even if the follow-up never runs. A DIFFERENT-lever change is allowed but flagged 'experiment_window_orthogonal' and logged as a page_change_event. Corrective types (ranking_recovery) and approval-gated tasks are never held. Re-opening the same URL+lever supersedes the prior experiment (clock reset). The 14d hold is for ATTRIBUTION only; a confirmation watch (outcome_loop.confirm) may keep monitoring past it without re-blocking same-lever edits. Levers are defined in cli/lib/experiments.js (LEVER_FOR_TYPE).
research_window.owner_override: To ship a held same-lever change now, deliberately re-run/deploy it — opening the new experiment supersedes the old window and resets the 14-day clock.

outcome_loop.note: Closes the business-outcome loop. After a ranking-affecting deploy, the executor-scheduled ranking_followup judges success on GSC CLICKS (primary), not SERP position. Position is a secondary diagnostic and the fallback signal when a page has fewer than min_baseline_clicks (too noisy to judge on clicks). Read by cli/lib/outcome_loop.js; every value is overridable via env (CLIENT_CLICK_DROP_RATIO, CLIENT_MIN_BASELINE_CLICKS, CLIENT_FOLLOWUP_DAYS, CLIENT_RECHECK_DAYS, CLIENT_REQUIRED_CONSECUTIVE_DEGRADED, CLIENT_MAX_WATCH_DAYS).

confirm.note: Debounce — NEVER act on a single dip (owner rule). A degraded reading starts a confirmation watch; only required_consecutive_degraded degraded checks within max_watch_days trigger auto-remediation. Re-checks run every recheck_days. The gate applies to BOTH responses (rollback and refresh).

outcome_loop.auto_remediation_note: On a CONFIRMED regression the ranking_followup auto-remediates with no human gate (sanctioned autonomous edges — the auditor should NOT flag these): metadata/link/performance-lever changes are auto-ROLLED BACK via deploy-rollback of the offending commit; content-lever changes are auto-REFRESHED by enqueuing a content_refresh task (blog_content lane — the content-refresh skill's Autonomous Pipeline Mode re-optimizes the page in place and republishes). If a rollback cannot proceed (no commit, already rolled back, or git-revert conflict), it falls back to filing a ranking_recovery task for a human. Corrective/unknown levers never auto-act.

homepage_canonical_money_terms.rule: The HOMEPAGE is the canonical ranking target for these head money terms. Do NOT create or push dedicated /services/* pages for them â€” that cannibalizes the homepage. Support the homepage instead: (1) risk=safe internal_link_opportunity tasks linking these anchors from topically-relevant blogs to the homepage, and (2) supporting blog posts (new_blog_post) that link up to the homepage. The keyword registry already maps all six to the homepage (page_type=home).

explicit_approval_queue.mechanism: high-risk pipeline Phase 1 (v2 task approve request)
explicit_approval_queue.description: Only require_explicit_approval task types are held here. They appear under 'needs_explicit_go' in the plan email and proceed only when the owner approves via Telegram. All other work proceeds automatically.
